news.title:
Indian airlines to operate over 23,000 weekly domestic flights from Mar 29.
This marks a 10% reduction compared to the same period last year.
news.summary:

This marks a 10% reduction compared to the same period last year.

news.title:
InterGlobe Aviation faces GST demand with interest and a INR 43 Cr penalty.
The issue pertains to unreceived services; company plans legal contest for FY19-25.
news.summary:
InterGlobe Aviation faces GST demand with interest and a INR 43 Cr penalty.
The issue pertains to unreceived services; company plans legal contest for FY19-25.

news.title:
IndiGo flags rising jet fuel costs and Middle East tensions affecting demand and airfares.
Fuel surcharges of Rs 425-2,300 introduced; other airlines like Air India and Akasa Air follow suit.
news.summary:
IndiGo flags rising jet fuel costs and Middle East tensions affecting demand and airfares.
Fuel surcharges of Rs 425-2,300 introduced; other airlines like Air India and Akasa Air follow suit.

news.title:
Nifty surged over 23,000; Sensex rose 1,681.7 pts as markets gained amid Iran-U.S. negotiation hopes.
InterGlobe Aviation, L&T, UltraTech Cement led gains; Coal India, Power Grid lagged; India VIX dropped 6.3%.
news.summary:
Nifty surged over 23,000; Sensex rose 1,681.7 pts as markets gained amid Iran-U.S. negotiation hopes.
InterGlobe Aviation, L&T, UltraTech Cement led gains; Coal India, Power Grid lagged; India VIX dropped 6.3%.

news.title:
IndiGo shares surged 5% as crude oil prices dropped significantly.
Jefferies forecasts a potential 48% increase for IndiGo's stock.
news.summary:

news.title:
IndiGo sees a relief rally as crude oil prices decline.
De-escalation in Iran-US tensions expected to curb input costs.

news.title:
Jefferies maintains Buy on InterGlobe Aviation with a target of Rs 6140.
Summer departures show 6–7% YoY growth; concerns over CASK due to West Asia crisis, fuel inflation.

news.title:
The 2026 Iran war impacted Indian stocks, with L&T, IndiGo, and HPCL facing declines due to disrupted oil supply, trade routes, and investor outflows.
L&T lost 20% over Middle East exposure risks, IndiGo dropped 16% from flight cancellations and fuel costs, while HPCL fell 25% amid crude price spikes.
news.summary:
The 2026 Iran war impacted Indian stocks, with L&T, IndiGo, and HPCL facing declines due to disrupted oil supply, trade routes, and investor outflows.
L&T lost 20% over Middle East exposure risks, IndiGo dropped 16% from flight cancellations and fuel costs, while HPCL fell 25% amid crude price spikes.

news.title:
HDFC Bank, NBCC, Indigo, Balkrishna Industries, and GR Infraprojects are key stocks to watch.
GR Infraprojects secures ₹2,440.9 crore NHAI order; Balkrishna Industries to issue ₹750 crore NCDs.
news.summary:

news.title:
IndiGo appoints Aloke Singh as Chief Strategy Officer effective April 6, 2026.
Singh, with 30 years aviation experience, will lead strategic planning and transformation.
news.summary:
IndiGo appoints Aloke Singh as Chief Strategy Officer effective April 6, 2026.
Singh, with 30 years aviation experience, will lead strategic planning and transformation.

news.title:
InterGlobe Aviation fell 5.5% to Rs 3,920 as a foreign brokerage cut its target price from Rs 6,000 to Rs 5,200 due to rising fuel costs and Middle East tensions.
Brokerage projects FY26 EBITDAR of Rs 13,700 cr, highlights IndiGo's market position, fuel surcharge, and robust cash balance despite 77.6% Q3 profit drop.
news.summary:
InterGlobe Aviation fell 5.5% to Rs 3,920 as a foreign brokerage cut its target price from Rs 6,000 to Rs 5,200 due to rising fuel costs and Middle East tensions.
Brokerage projects FY26 EBITDAR of Rs 13,700 cr, highlights IndiGo's market position, fuel surcharge, and robust cash balance despite 77.6% Q3 profit drop.

Airlines argue the mandate will force airfare hikes for passengers.

news.title:
Govt removes domestic airfare caps, allowing airlines to adjust costs.
IndiGo and others may raise fares as operating costs escalate.

news.title:
West Asia conflict raises challenges for airlines, including airspace curbs, high fuel costs, and surging insurance premiums.
IndiGo, with 18% revenue from West Asia, faces fuel cost pressure but can offset via its 64% domestic market share.
news.summary:
